name: wazuh
explanation: Wazuh is a host intrusion detection system (HIDS). The Datadog integration is logs-only: the spec defines a single `logs` block expecting the user to declare a UDP listener (`type: udp`, `port: <PORT>`) for Wazuh to forward syslog-style events into. There is no Agent metric check at all — `datadog_checks/wazuh/` contains no `wazuh.py`, only `__about__.py`, `__init__.py`, and a `data/conf.yaml.example`. There is no upstream service to probe over the network from the Agent: the Agent passively receives forwarded events on a port the user has chosen and must configure on the Wazuh side as well. Auto-config in the network-probe sense does not apply.
notes: Logs-only integration with no instances block in spec. Same shape as zeek and zscaler_private_access (and broadly pattern N — the integration is a log-shipping tile, not a probeable service).
